text.title: Process Mining and Intelligent Automation Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the process-mining-automation methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (profile logs): Schema completeness + case-id quality check; reject if below threshold.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the profile logs gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (discover): Run miner; emit as-is BPMN.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the discover gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (conformance check): Token replay; identify deviations + fitness score.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the conformance check gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (score candidates): Apply matrix; total score with breakdown.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the score candidates gate.

rule.statement: Step 5 (assess verdict and roi): Pick verdict per candidate; estimate ROI.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the assess verdict and roi gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions for process-mining-automation do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
